_comment: Video pool for the daily social autoposter. pick_daily_video.py rotates through `pool`; video_publisher.py posts and then RETIRES each posted clip (removes its entry here and deletes the MP4). Refresh the pool from the factory vault (~/videos/_vault).

pool.caption: Your old MacBook loses ~$25 in value every month it sits. Sell in July: $480. Wait till October: $400. Same laptop — only the date changed. Lock today's price at purchasingcorp.com
The AI boom just made your junk drawer valuable. Memory prices are up eighty nine percent this year. See what yours is worth → purchasingcorp.com
New PCs just jumped twenty percent overnight. We paid three forty, same day, for a used tower. See what yours is worth → purchasingcorp.com
That old graphics card is suddenly worth a fortune. The new flagship is pushing five thousand dollars. See what yours is worth → purchasingcorp.com
Those old RAM sticks are literally money right now. We paid a hundred twenty for a bag of old modules. See what yours is worth → purchasingcorp.com
Phone prices are climbing, and here's why it helps you. Top payout, six forty, same day. See what yours is worth → purchasingcorp.com
Samsung just unveiled the new Fold. Cash out your old one. We paid five twenty for a used foldable, same day. See what yours is worth → purchasingcorp.com

Everyone wants the new smart glasses. Cash out what you don't wear. We paid ninety five for a pile of old wearables. See what yours is worth → purchasingcorp.com

Cracked, dead, water damaged. In this market it still pays. We paid ninety for a cracked, dead phone. See what yours is worth → purchasingcorp.com
That old tablet buried in a drawer is a stack of cash. We paid two forty for a used tablet. See what yours is worth → purchasingcorp.com

Those old mining GPUs are back in serious demand. We paid nine hundred for a bundle of cards. See what yours is worth → purchasingcorp.com
Open your junk drawer. It's hiding hundreds of dollars. The average drawer we buy pays four thirty. See what yours is worth → purchasingcorp.com

This phone wouldn't even turn on. Guy assumed it was e-waste. I sent him four ten that same afternoon. See what yours is worth → purchasingcorp.com
